entry.title: Re: SAP EWM Addition to stock putaway strategy
entry.content: @Jelena_Perfiljeva: you find this and similar documents at http://help.sap.com/EWM > see in Screen section Additional Information > How-To Guides. Best regards, Matthias

entry.title: Re: [SAP PO 7.5] Problem with adding wrapper element name with dot (.) in REST receiver adapter
entry.content:
We have the same problem with a REST receiver adapter.

The outer XML tag is a RFC response message in the format RFCName.Response.
But only tag <Response> was created. But we need <RFCName.Response>.

Does anybody has a solution for this?

entry.title: Re: Could not convert from '' to 'USD' on '01.01.2025'. P...
entry.content:
Organizational-Plant have currency .

Currency table have category - Actual 

Rate ,rate type

entry.title: Re: Why does a restore fail with error message 'LogAndDataIncompatible'?

Hi,

Thank You for your solution 

It worked for me

entry.title: Re: SCIM Connector on CIS in BTP trial version
entry.content: Just a follow-up question on this please. Is there any other way to connect a 3rd party application to IPS as a source system without the SCIM connector? Or is it then not possible to connect a 3rd party system to IPS without purchasing a license for IAG to get the SCIM connector? Or am I misunderstanding?

entry.title: Re: Adopting General Display Name
entry.content:
Hi @S0025138380 ,

It is possible to reach what you want. You need to use a business rule that implements exactly what you wish.

We had the same request from our business and created a business rule that sets the Display Name to Preferred First Name + Preferred Last Name if those are available.

Afterwards we set the Name Format to be the Display Name.

I hope this help.

Best regards

entry.title: Re: DM Deprecated Services in Production Processes
entry.content:
Deprecated APIs are normally still usable up to 12 months after deprecation. However, there are certain cases were APIs are decommissioned even before this period. See this help page for details: https://help.sap.com/docs/PRODUCT_ID/e56a6c50d31541ea826021dc8e721a53/5cbfda5a9efe4e97a3e24ddaf7ec5c16.html?state=PRODUCTION&version=Cloud&locale=en-US 

In general, I would highly recommend replacing them sooner than later. Also read the release notes carefully if the decommissioning is mentioned, also in the API Hub change log you normally find the deprecation release to calculate the decommissioning release.

entry.title: 在维护“设置支付事务的银行确定”时，银行子账户不能维护，保存后为空

entry.title: Re: Missing Change Request Type Selection in Fiori "Manag...

Hi Jose,

MDG FIORI app uses business activity 'BPF1'. Please check the CR types which are relevant for this business activity.

Regards,

Saravanan

entry.title: Subscription Failed error in configuring SAP Build Apps in Trial Account
entry.content: I tried several times but I am facing the same issue while Configuring Build Apps ie, Subscription Failed due to SaaS subscription issue. I even made a new trial account and created subaccount to configure but the issue remains same.

entry.title: SAP Build Apps Issue - High Impact
entry.content:
Dear Members,

The Build Apps is now being giving issues from last week.

I was receiving the 403 error so I tried all possible approaches and there was no way to resolve it.

Currently, I deleted the sub account and tried to access the Build Apps with a new Sub Account.

The installation failed, stating unable to subscribe to the SaaS application.

When I opened it in the Subscriptions of the sub account, still it's in the free plan but failing to install the application. Below screenshots would help you to understand the issue in detail.

I had a demo in the coming week and lost everything at the moment. IT would be helpful if someone can provide me a good solution.

Thanks and Regards,

Ananthu RB

entry.title: Why can I find an employee/user in "hire date correction" field but not elsewhere in EC?
entry.content:
I have a strange situation, where I need to delete a user from EC, because the flow from recruiting to onboarding did not go smooth, due to change of hire date in the future. The employee had initially start date February 1, but the actual first day was February 10. Somehow my team managed to change the hiring-date from 2/1/2025 to 2/10/2025, but then the employee "disappeared" in EC.

We searched after the employee in recruiting, org.chart, search-bar, onboarding and reporting, but we could not find the person until we used "hire date correction" - then the employees name showed but without much data. 

We now want to start the process from scratch with this employee (meaning creating the employee again), but first we must delete the persons data entirely from EC. Due to error message from SF stating "employee data already exists", when we try to create the employee again, this is not possible.

To sum up: How can we delete this persons data for good, when we can't find the employee in purge/data retention management?

entry.title: Re: Routing: Alternative Sequence & Parallel Sequence in SAP
entry.content:
Really nice write up, thank you so much for sharing!

One comment: when working with sequences it is crucial to always consider the restrictions mentioned in SAP note 41546. Perhaps you could add this?

entry.title: Re: How to redeem a campaign code to get a free SAP Certification exam attempt and schedule your exa
entry.content:
Hi Professor @David_Chaviano 

 

i finished the course SAP Certified Associate - Database Administrator - SAP HANA

and sent the screenshot  to digitalskills@sap.com. But, I haven't received the campaign code, as yet?  Any suggestions would be appreciated

 

 

 

Professor, I beg you to help me get a free campaign code so that I can take the exam. I have finished the course and sent many emails to Digital Skills and sent here the details of the end of the course, but the code has not been sent to me. I hope you can help me.

entry.title: Disable SAP GUI Security Pop-up when upload UI5 App in SAP
entry.content:
Introduction:  It begins innocently enough. We run the report /UI5/UI5_REPOSITORY_LOAD, hoping to capture all the UI5 changes in SAP Transport. The report prompts us for a file location path and Transport request, and we obligingly provide the necessary details.

But little do we know, this is where the excitement begins. As we proceed, the SAP GUI Security pop-up starts making its presence felt – and felt, and felt, and felt. For each page, it politely (but persistently) asks for permission, ignoring our pleas to 'Remember My Decision.' It's like trying to reason with a determined toddler!

The result? A thrilling game of pop-up whack-a-mole that can last up to an hour or more, depending on the number of UI5 files that need to be uploaded. And just when you think it's all over, another pop-up appears, taunting you with its presence.

But wait, there's more! Just when you thought you'd finally tamed the pop-up beast, it rears its head again. If you have the misfortune of changing the file path for the next upload, the SAP GUI Security pop-up will gleefully start asking for permissions all over again.

Isn't it exhilarating to repeat the same process over and over? But fear not, we have a clever solution to gently tame this situation.

Enter the SAP GUI Logon Pad to vanquish the pop-up monster, simply:

  1. Open the SAP GUI Logon Pad

  2. Click on Options

Next click on Security → Security Configuration

Click on ‘Open Security Configuration' button under 'Security Module’ Box.

And choose ‘Default Action’ as ‘Allow’.

Boom! Issue resolved. The SAP GUI Security pop-up has been vanquished!
From this day forward, you shall upload your UI5 files in peace, without the incessant interruptions of those pesky pop-ups. It's a beautiful feeling, isn't it?
And don't worry, this solution is completely reversible. Once you've finished your upload, you can simply revert the settings back to their original state, and all will be right with the world again.

 

This ends the functionality.
Appreciate you stopping by and reading the article! Your comments, suggestions, and shares are greatly appreciated.

entry.title: The YELLOW exclamation point in the projection of SAP HANA Graphical Calculation View.
entry.content:
Hi experts,

Drop table into the projection of SAP HANA Graphical Calculation View, all field are displayed with yellow exclamation point as below :

Tks.

entry.title: Re: ABAP RAP - Service binding issue - no data when launching ADT preview for a node
entry.content: Hello Everyone, @Naveen13k like said .It's sort parameter problem if you change sort parameter or " presentationVariant: [{ sortOrder: [{ by: 'TravelId', direction: #ASC }] }] } " change parameter TravelID -> TravelId. You can solve like this.

entry.title: Re: SAC - Block/overwrite old bookmarks
entry.content:
Hi Eric - I came across the same issue recently. Unfortunately I couldn't find a solution.

I am not even sure if it is possible to find out which users have set their own (i.e. not global) bookmarks so that they can be proactively informed.

In an ideal scenario, an admin could see and then edit/delete private bookmarks for exactly the (not uncommon) scenario you described.

entry.title: Re: Reimagining digital learning as an integrated part of a Digital Adoption Platform with SAP &&&&
entry.content:
@laurenfisher404 

Hi Lauren, 

ongoing contracts will of course be honored and all existing customers can use SAP Enable Now, cloud edition for as long as their contract runs. The future solution for digital adoption is certainly WalkMe. 

Since the new WalkMe offering for digital learning is currently being developed, I can't answer your questions about transition options at this time. SAP will consider these scenarios during development and provide transition options for existing content. 

More details will follow once the new WalkMe offering takes shape and I will update this blog to share them.

entry.title: Re: Partial HU packing with fiori APP
entry.content: Any recommendation on this ? We are facing similar problem, drag and drop works in GUI but not in FIORI

entry.title: Re: SAP Customer Data Cloud Back and Restore
entry.content:
Hi @Rvanjari I'm not aware of the offline backup and restore tools in CDC, but you may check out some online tools like "Copy Configuration" to backup your configurations online in CDC. 

Further more, our CDC experts created  "sap-customer-data-cloud-toolkit", which supports more features like import/export of email/sms templates and Copy Configuration Extended. 

Hope it helps 🙂

entry.title: Generate a custom pdf in S/4HANA with metadata from S/4HANA & non-SAP systems
entry.content: hi, can we generate a custom pdf document in S4H with metadata from S4 & other non-SAP applications w/o any outputs & transaction specific (i.e. SO, PO, WO etc.)??

entry.title: Re: DCI cannot be set for the item.
entry.content: Probably this is some custom message, that is specific to your system only. Double click on that error message and check what is its number. If it starts from Z or Y then its a custom error.

entry.title: Issue when deploying simple app to BTP using 'cf push'
entry.content:
Hey everyone,

I’m new to BTP apps, and honestly, I’m about to lose it—so I really hope someone can help!

I’m using BAS (Business Application Studio) in case that’s relevant. My goal is simple: deploy a basic Fiori app with a blank page that displays a list of data from a web service. Everything works fine when running it in BAS for testing.

I’ve explored BTP in the past, but due to the complexity around deployments, manifests, YAML files, routes, and MTA, I put it on hold until things hopefully became more stable. Now, I’m trying again and facing deployment issues.

The Problem:

I attempted to deploy the app using cf push. I also tried using MTA, but for some reason, the HTML5 app is not being deployed. The ultimate goal is to add this app to S/4HANA Cloud Public via Launchpad.

When checking the logs with:

 

cf logs <prj_name> --recent

 

I see the following errors (in red):

 

   2025-02-13T06:10:09.11+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] ERR sh: 1: tsc: not found
   2025-02-13T06:10:09.12+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] ERR npm notice
   2025-02-13T06:10:09.12+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] ERR npm notice New major version of npm available! 10.7.0 -> 11.1.0
   2025-02-13T06:10:09.12+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] ERR npm notice Changelog: https://github.com/npm/cli/releases/tag/v11.1.0
   2025-02-13T06:10:09.12+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] ERR npm notice To update run: npm install -g npm@11.1.0
   2025-02-13T06:10:09.12+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] ERR npm notice

 

It seems there are two major issues here:

1. TSC (TypeScript Compiler) not found

2. NPM version inconsistency

 

What I’ve Tried (Without Success)

1️⃣Fixing the tsc: not found Issue

Modified package.json to move TypeScript from devDependencies to dependencies:

 

Modify package.json
"dependencies": {
  "typescript": "^5.2.2"  // Añádelo aquí
},
"devDependencies": {
  
}

 

Installed TypeScript globally:

 

npm install typescript

 

Added a post-install script in package.json:

 

"scripts": {
"postinstall": "npm install -g typescript"
}

 

Verified TypeScript installation: 

 

npx tsc --version #returns 5.7.3

 

Despite all of this, cf push still throws the tsc: not found error.

 

2️⃣Fixing the NPM Version Issue

Updated NPM with:

 

npm install -g npm@11.1.0

 

However, running npm -v still shows the old version.

To fix this, I tried:

 

export PATH=$(npm prefix -g)/bin:$PATH

 

After this, npm -v shows the correct version (11.1.0).

But the errors still persist exactly the same after redeployment.

 

3️⃣ Other Things I’ve Tried (Also Failed)

✅Specified the Node.js version in package.json:

 

"engines": {
    "node": "20.x"
  }​

 

✅Created a manifest.yml to reduce memory size (not directly related but worth trying).

✅Deleted the failed app to free up space in BTP.

✅Completely removed and reinstalled node_modules:

 

rm -rf node_modules package-lock.json
npm cache clean --force
npm install

 

Yet, nothing has worked.

I’ve already gone through these articles, but they didn’t solve my issue:

• 🔗 Tips for Running CAP Node.js in SAP BTP

• 🔗 Error While Deploying CAP Project to CF Trial

My Questions

1. How can I ensure Cloud Foundry uses the correct Node.js and NPM versions during deployment?

2. Why does cf push fail with tsc: not found even though TypeScript is installed globally?

3. What’s the proper way to deploy a simple Fiori app (HTML5) to BTP and register it in S/4HANA Cloud Public via Launchpad?

I’d really appreciate any guidance—thanks in advance! 🚀

entry.title: Restricting Enterprise Search - S_ESH_CONN
entry.content: Trying to restrict the enterprise search in Fiori but is getting bypassed by something, could be a parameter setting? S_ESH_CONN was already restricted to select connector ID's, no other roles provides this object.

entry.title: Community Member Spotlight: Roberta Fernandes - February 2025 Edition
entry.content:
You might have seen our first three iterations of recognizing active authors (one / two / three)  in SAP Community. I am thrilled to share our third interview with @RobertaFernande with you which I conducted recently. Read more about her background, her experience with organizing community events and what motivates her to contribute to SAP Community! 

Marco Santos (MS): Hello, Roberta! Let's dive right in with the first question: how and when did you join the SAP Community?

Roberta: Well, it was around 2017 when I first got involved with the SAP community. At that time, we were doing an update on SAP HANA while I was finishing a project at Caixa in Brasília. Marcel, who conducted our training, was a huge advocate for the SAP Community. He was always organizing events and really was an influential figure within the SAP space. Back then, not just anyone could organize events; it was mostly reserved for SAP Mentors and SAP Champions.

Marcel saw potential in me. He approached me and said, "Roberta, I think you should participate more. You always bring positivity and are very involved in the events as a participant." His encouragement inspired me, and I started to join events alongside him to learn the ropes. That's really how my journey began in 2017.

I continued my involvement in 2018 and 2019, even taking part in events hosted in Rio and Campinas. In 2018, for instance, I was at the entrance of an SAP event in São Paulo, managing the reception. From there, I developed a real passion for these gatherings. What I truly enjoy is seeing people come together with a desire to learn. It's the best part about these events—witnessing a shared eagerness for growth and knowledge.


MS: What does the SAP Community mean to you?

Roberta: To me, the SAP Community represents knowledge and innovation. For example, in my case, I first encountered Central Finance at a community event, and today I work with Central Finance. I've been involved with it for about three years now.

The first time I learned about Central Finance was in 2019 when I saw Rodrigo Mantovani from SAP speaking about this module. During his talk, he discussed how it centralizes accounting processes. That event sparked my interest and prompted me to start studying this topic. It was an innovation for me, and I believe it serves as an innovation for others as well. It motivates people to seek knowledge, which is incredibly valuable. Through the SAP Community, you stay informed about developments more rapidly.

MS: Beyond being oneself and gaining knowledge, what else inspires you as a person?

Roberta: I think it's seeing the expressions on people's faces when they are engaged, you know? It's really amazing. People, for instance, in Recife, you could see them looking so intently, as if they wanted to absorb every bit of knowledge, to carry that knowledge with them, you know? I find it incredible because, sometimes, in just 40 minutes, a person can gain insights that last a lifetime, you know?

MS: That sounds great! Can you tell us a bit more about your experience with SAP products and your background?

Roberta: Of course. I began working with SAP in 2003. Before that, I was an accountant and had experience with different ERPs. I worked at Fox, which had an internal ERP system. In 1995, someone mentioned a system that was quite similar to SAP, sparking my curiosity. I started exploring companies that used this system.

I joined Kaeser Kompressor, a German company, to serve as an interface between Brazil and Germany. Initially, I worked as a user, learning processes like, invoice entry, and accounting. Back then, Brazil hadn’t fully localized the software yet. This gap prompted me to dive deeper and explore more about SAP.

I found a forum, back in 2003, and through it, I met someone who owned a company that later became part of Capgemini. He recognized my interest and potential, suggesting I join their SAP academy. Wagner Guimarães gave me the opportunity to become a certified SAP CO Consultant. I took various courses, focusing on SAP FI and PS, and learned about Central Finance. My expertise lies in financial and accounting processes. Although I'm generally a quiet and reserved person, when I encounter an error, I am relentless in finding the solution. People often quoted, “There’s a world beyond SAP,” but I am passionately dedicated to this field.

SAP has opened doors for me in multiple ways. It not only exposed me to diverse cultures but also significantly impacted my life. Coming from a very poor family in Brazil, as the oldest of eight siblings, SAP allowed me to support many people. I'm committed to giving back by sharing knowledge and introducing more people to the world of SAP.

I've been in the SAP ecosystem for about 22 years. Obviously, I won't be working another 22 years, so it's crucial to train new professionals. During my last project with Caixa Econômica, I mentored five new SAP FI consultants. This is why community initiatives, such as the recent event held at a university in Campinas, are so vital. They introduce SAP to new audiences who may not be familiar with it and might inspire future consultants. It's an exciting prospect.

MS: You've organized many events. Is there a particular highlight you'd like to share with us?

Roberta: Yes, the happiest day of my life was when I got involved with an event at PUC last year, on July 27th. It was a massive event, with 300 people signing up. I was extremely nervous because I was in charge of everything. At one point, someone turned to me and said, "Roberta, everything's going as planned. Go live." It was incredible to see the event unfold. Imagine 300 people gathering for breakfast, checking in, receiving their shirts. Everything went smoothly, thanks to the entire team, of course. We had 8 volunteers, which really helped. It was amazing to watch the footage afterwards. Sure, there were some hiccups, but seeing everything mostly perfect was rewarding.

MS: The anxieties we experience before such events often disappear when we see everything going according to plan with so many people involved.

Roberta: Exactly.

MS: Organizing such events isn’t an everyday occurrence. Looking towards the future, do you have any plans for your role in the community over the next few years?

Roberta: I do have a goal. It's not so much a target, but a personal ambition to speak more. I have a lot of knowledge, but I still have fears to overcome. I'd like to be a SAP Mentor, but I often only share my knowledge in small groups. I'd love to share it with larger groups, like 300 people, but I still have a lot of work to do on this personal challenge.

MS: What other aspects are you looking to gain from participating in the SAP Community?

Roberta: I believe, Marco, that one of the key aspects is networking. It's about creating an impact on the lives of others, and I find that incredibly rewarding. When someone signs up and is exposed to knowledge, innovation, and the open-mindedness of the community, it leaves a lasting impression. It’s essential for people to stay engaged, whether it’s from 8 in the morning until 6 or even 8 in the evening. They choose knowledge, and that, to me, is the greatest impact and what I love most. Every time we hold an event, and people attend and listen to the speakers, it's a significant achievement.

It's amazing when you think about it. People could be at the beach or in a park, yet they choose to participate, seated and focused. At our recent event in São Paulo, even though it went until 7 PM, the turnout was impressive, and people stayed until the end.

It’s fulfilling to know that what we deliver keeps people engaged. At events, whether in São Paulo or Recife, attendees don’t leave; they stay and absorb the experience.

MS: How did you come up with the idea to organize events?

Roberta: It's interesting because I really love SAP. The idea of organizing events initially came from the desire to expand my knowledge and to become better known by others. I think that's very important in any community.

It's something people often do without expecting any return, but in reality, they gain a lot. When I talk about gains, I'm not referring to monetary ones but to the value of building a name for yourself and expanding your knowledge and network. There's a real-life example of this: someone called me about a presentation by Rogério Falleiros from São Paulo. They wanted to review it for their presentation. This shows how valuable connecting with others and sharing knowledge can be.

MS: Now, for the final question. Do you have anything else you'd like to share with me or the SAP Community?

Roberta: I'd like to see people engage more. Sometimes, there are older consultants who hesitate to share their knowledge because they fear someone might take their place.

We're all just passing through, after all. So, why not pass on knowledge to others? Engagement should involve large-scale knowledge transfer, whether through platforms like SAP Tech or in our daily interactions. It's important to share what we know.

People should share without fearing they'll lose their jobs. Specialists and experts will always be needed. We need to develop more knowledgeable individuals, knowledgeable about processes, SAP, and new technologies. There's always so much to learn.

MS: That's right. Well, to wrap up the interview, thank you very much for taking the time to talk with me and share your experiences and journey with the SAP Community.

Roberta: Thank you too.

entry.title: How can I extend/increase the period of HANA load history?
entry.content:
By default, HANA load history is saved for approximately 12 days. How can I extend or increase the period of HANA load history?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How can I extend/increase the period of HANA load his...
entry.content:
The load history collection can be configured using the following SAP HANA parameters:

  • global.ini -> [resource_tracking] -> load_monitor_granularity
  • global.ini -> [resource_tracking] -> load_monitor_max_samples

Please adjust/increase these parameters according to your business requirements.
You can find more details about these parameters in the following documents:

  • 2762918 - Short period of load history in HANA system
  • 2222110 - FAQ: SAP HANA Load History -> Section 3. How can the load history collection be configured?

entry.title: Re: BO and Oracle 23ai support
entry.content:
Hi,

At the moment Oracle 23ai (Oracle DB version 23) is still not supported by SAP, but we are working on to release it as soon as possible.

Below to SAP documents are constantly updated when it is appropriate to do that:

2799958 - Oracle Database 23ai: Preliminary Information about the next Oracle Database Release

3546163 - Will Oracle 23ai be certified with SAP BusinessObjects platform ?

entry.title: Is local class supported for a global class in BTP ABAP enviroment?
entry.content:
In BTP ABAP environment,  I would like to create a local class under a global class (similar with SE24 in on-premise system ). However, it shows syntax error.

If it is supported, can anyone provide the sample code?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Incorrect "House Bank" and "House Bank Account" in Ch...
entry.content: Dear Expert!

entry.title: Re: RAP Business Events with Advanced Event Mesh [2]: Creating custom business events
entry.content:
Hi @TimoMaier ,
Thanks for the quick response.
With deep payloads , I am able to achieve header and item level payloads, something like this.

{
document:100
{ item : 10, quantity : 1, changedby " ABC }
}

But, I need to have the payload with multiple line items in a single event that triggers on lineitem change.
{
document:100
{ item : 10,quantity : 1,changedby : ABC }
{ item : 20, quantity : 1, changedby " ABC }
}
Any suggestion is greatly appreciated. Thanks in advance.

entry.content:
In Windows when we right click to see the task manager ,we see processes within the same .

So the lower limit of 1000 tasks means in Windows Task Manager the 1000 apps running right ,and this takes place in the VM where Commerce Cloud is hosted ?

entry.title: Re: SCIM Connector on CIS in BTP trial version
entry.content:
@VedettevanKleef 
you will have to use IPS Proxy for external connection using SCIM.

entry.title: Re: Disable SAP GUI Security Pop-up when upload UI5 App in SAP
entry.content:
Do you want to know the changes for SAP UI5 Apps or you own apps?

Do you want make sure your extension still can work or why do you want to know this?

entry.title: YMCR. SCope - add admin change - configuration item is getting erased on save.
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are using one continous cycle with no task.   We create change request  and tried to add admin change  by giving production configuration item. But after saving configuration item is getting erased and throwing error and add product id again. 

When we try to add general change .. we don't see this issue.  I understand that general change is for non-sap activities and so scope check is not happening. But for admin change this scope check for production system is happening and thus throwing error.   We are facing this issue after recent upgrade to SP19. Earlier we are able to create admin change with continous cycle.

Can you help me how to bypass this scope check for admin change similar like general change in change request.

Thank you,

Raghav

entry.title: Re: Global value in sap mdg fpm : mv_fpm_edit_mode
entry.content:
Hi Karthik,

UI Building Blocks (UIBBs) that are provided by the Floorplan Manager (FPM) UI framework , so-called "General UIBBs" (GUIBBs), for example a Form, List, or a Tree GUIBB, do get the information whether they are in display or edit mode via the parameter IV_EDIT_MODE in the feeder class method GET_DATA.
Free-style UIBBs that are not provided by the FPM, but have been created by an application area or a customer, do not automatically get this information as they do not have feeder methods, but only the methods of the WD interface IF_FPM_UI_BUILDING_BLOCK. However, such free-style UIBBs (and by the way GUIBBs as well) can always get the information about their edit mode state by calling method IF_FPM~GET_UIBB_EDIT_MODE, passing over the UIBB instance key and the respective window (interface view).

I hope these explanations will help a little bit...

Best regards,
Jens

entry.title: Re: RAP Business Events with Advanced Event Mesh [2]: Creating custom business events
entry.content:
Hi @Padmini2709,

This can still be done with deep payloads.

The resulting payload would then look something like this:

{
      document:100,
      items: [
             { item : 10, quantity : 1, changedby : ABC },
             { item : 20, quantity : 1, changedby " ABC }
      ]
}

For this the composition in the payload just has to be adjusted to 0..*
For example like this:
address : composition [0..*] of z_addr_changed_payload_addr;

entry.title: Re: SAP Java Buildpack 2: Jakarta-Based, Fresh and New!

entry.title: Adobe SFP Table-Header on Page 2 with hidden columns
entry.content:
Hello Gurus,

I have created a table in an SFP form in which columns are hidden in certain constellations. This works very well. However, hiding the columns is implemented in the INITIALIZE script function. If the table is now displayed over several pages, the table heading moves (Page 1 is ok, Page 2 header shift due to hidden columns to the right)

Is the INITIALIZE event only processed once per table?

Is there another script function that is processed on every page?

I have attached a few screenshots of page 1 and page 2.

Thank you in advance for your help

Best regards

Frank

entry.title: Re: Introducing Joule on Hands-on Practice Sandbox

entry.title: Importance Goods Receipt Indicator Firm in Account Assignment Categories
entry.content:
This blog highlights the significance of the Goods Receipt Indicator Firm in the Account Assignment Category. It can be applied to any type of Account Assignments with various combinations. For example, if needed for cost center K, create an additional indicator referencing K and ensure the GR Indicator Firm is checked according to business requirements. Similarly, if required for services, create an AAC referencing D and check the GR Indicator Firm as per business needs.

Step 1: Create AAC with reference to Cost center (K)

SPRO --> Materials Management -> Purchasing -> Account Assignment -> Maintain Account Assignmen Categories

Select AAC K and navigate to Details

Check the GR Indicator Firm

Select AAC R and navigate to Details 

Uncheck GR Indicator Firm

Step 2: Define Combination of Item Categories/Account Assignment Categories

SPRO --> Materials Management -> Purchasing -> Account Assignment -> Define Combination of Item Categories/Account Assignment Categories

Step 3: Create Purchase Order

Two line items created one with AAC K (GR Ind. Firm Checked) and the other with AAC R (GR Ind. Firm Unchecked)

In Item Details, Under Delivery Tab for line item 10

GR Check box greyed out, which indicates GR is mandatory for this Account assignment category K and can not be modified.

In Item Details, Under Delivery Tab for line item 20

GR Check box is set to modifiable, which indicates GR is either optional/mandatory for this Account assignment category R, according to the business requirements clients may change this indicator.

If business do not want to check this Goods Receipt check box by default, you can uncheck this at Account Assignment Category configuration.

entry.title: Re: LTMC Process SAP S/4HANA – Simple Steps.
entry.content: Excellent way of explaining every step and important points in detailed. This can give clarity to even non sap users as well.  Great job done !

entry.title: Re: BP Transaction new tab under company code using BDT :...
entry.content:
@shubham_banerjee 

To update Lfa1 / LFB1 entry from BP Tcode 

use below BDT adapter class method 


          cvi_bdt_adapter=>set_current_bp_cc_data(
             EXPORTING
               i_table_name =    lc_tab_lfb1              " Table Name
               i_data_new   =    lt_lfb1[]
               i_validate   = 'X'              " Validate Data Automatically
             RECEIVING
             r_messages   = DATA(lt_message_lfb1)             " Return Table

 

Where lt_lfb1 will hold updated entry .

there are more  Set* methods in adapter class cvi_bdt_adapter to update other relivant table and can be call Custom BDT FM logic .

 

Regards ,

Akshay

entry.title: Re: Which KPI's do you have in software development?
entry.content: I would like to add another question to that topic 😉 Do you measure/apply any KPI for your Software Architecture (e.g. Fitness Function, Modularity Score, Metrics based on tools like ArchUnit, etc.)?

entry.title: Re: Import substances of own "regulatory lists" to SAP S/...
entry.content:
Hi Christoph,
long time no see 🙂

I have seen the blogs attached by you, but unfortunately they do not really help out.

On a SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (so always the latest version which is now 2502) on level of a compliance requirement which uses the pattern substance list, there is the possibility to maintain manually the listed substances that are relevant for this list. This is a manual process. The CPE even offers to import compliance requirements via a template, but not to offer something similar to import/populate the substances.
Even the API list does not cover this...

Cheerio
Harry

entry.title: Identifying if third party notification variant is active in job monitoring scenario in SOLMAN
entry.content:
Identifying if third party notification variant is active in job monitoring scenario in solution manager


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Identifying if third party notification variant is ac...
entry.content:
To check runtime configuration for certain Monitoring Object, you can review Alerting Directory Browser

##2349396    How to access "Alerting Directory Browser" in Solution Manager

  1. From the application filter by Monitoring Use Case = JOB_MON then apply
  2. Select affected Monitoring object from the list and click on the object itself
  3. Select Alerts tab and choose affected alert
  4. Then below you will see Third Party Components tab which will show runtime configuration

entry.title: Re: Understanding how email template works in Flexible Workflow

Nice blog is there way we can add CC email id's?

Regards

Karthik

entry.title: Re: SAP DM Monitor Production Processes
entry.content:
thanks to the SAP Support Team, we found the issue and the solution. Issue was the certificate, generated by the SAP cloud (App: Configure Production Connectivity): 

it didn't contain the entire certificate chain.
A regeneration of the shown certificate solved the problem.

Best Regards,
Michael

Kavita

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Work Zone (standard) and visibility using {...
entry.content:
Resolved with help of SAP Support team.

It turned out that that the UI5 version used in the workzone was different than version set up as minimal in the app.  This somehow made the visibility properties and other stuff defined in the fiori tools come out  scrambled. 

SAP Support brought to our attention KBA 0003242190, which advises how to to change the SAPUI5 version for the custom SAPUI5 apps that are running in the CF Portal service or CF Work Zone. As per instructions, we added the following in the manifest file:

 

"sap.platform.cf": {
"ui5VersionNumber": "same version as the SAPUI min version of the app"
},

 

This resolved our issue.

entry.title: How make Operation Number default while doing time confirmation screen in SAP Work Manager

as of now , we have to choose in list selection .

KV

entry.title: Re: Couldn't subscribe to SAP Build Apps. Try again.
entry.content: I've been having the same problem for two days now

entry.title: Re: Walkthrough SAP Cloud ALM Process Authoring
entry.content:
Hi Adrian,

It’s not entirely clear if the issue is related to the assigned roles, so here are a couple of scenarios to check:

  1. If you see the Process Authoring tile and can create Custom Solution Processes:

    • You likely have the necessary roles.
    • In this case, the issue might be that you are in the Solution Value Flow tab.
    • Simply switch to the Solution Process Flow tab, and you should then be able to copy the displayed process flow diagram.
    • (Please ensure that your Solution Process is not a custom solution process.)

  2. If you don’t see the Process Authoring tile or can’t create a Custom Solution Process Flow:

    • This indicates a role issue.
    • Open the User Management application, select your user, and assign the Process Author role. ( The below screenshot might help here.)
    • If the User Management application isn’t visible, an administrator will need to handle this step.

FYI: Regarding the roles in the Projects application, they are SAP Activate Roadmap roles and not directly related to the User Roles.

Can you please confirm if this helps?

Best Regards,

Mohamed.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA 2022: New BAdI to influence MRP elements in MRP Live and Classic MRP
entry.content:
Hi Caetano,
I have a tecnical question.
I'am using Badi "PPH_SUPPLY_DEMAND_LIST" to influence sales requirements, to remove them from production planning when they are blocked (credit block or special delivery)..

I copied the example code in the class.
I have the internal table but after the "insert into" statement the table is empty

--my code--
DECLARE lt_mdps_del TABLE LIKE :ct_supplydemanditemlist;

* Fill local table
INSERT INTO :lt_mdps_del( SELECT * FROM :ct_supplydemanditemlist__in__ );

after this I try to update the field "ProcessingKey" of table "lt_mdps_del" but is empty


Are there any options to set or any other precautions provided by this Badi?
Thanks for the support
Stefano

entry.title: Re: Is local class supported for a global class in BTP ABAP enviroment?
entry.content:
Example (see also official ABAP documentation examples):

CLASS lcl_app DEFINITION
    FINAL
    CREATE PRIVATE.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    CLASS-METHODS create
      RETURNING
        VALUE(result) TYPE REF TO lcl_app.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS lcl_app IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD create.
    result = NEW lcl_app( ).
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

entry.title: Re: Adding a Value Help for Custom Business Object fields

Data will only show up if we maintain something in the Primary Data Source which is the Custom CBO. Which defeats the purpose because we only want the data from the standard CDS View to be used as Value Help.

entry.title: Re: Storing Output Documents in SAP S/4HANA

Hello @dirk_heede,

Regards,

Markus

entry.title: An error occurred while operating a TECO production order using T-Code CO02 or COHV
entry.content:
Subject: [SAP System] Error Report - [Short Dump ID] Dear SAP Support, I am experiencing an issue in our SAP system that I have been unable to resolve. Below is the information requested for your assistance:

1. **Short Dump Description:** - [The production order status cannot be reversed due to the change of the factory in the middle of the sales demand transmission]

 

2. **System Log:** - [Attach the saved system log file here]

3. **Source Code:** - [* step 6: Umsetzung in externes Format
  PERFORM com2_convert_instances_int_ext
   USING l_ibinwa_tab l_instance_tab2.

* step 5: read the structure
  LOOP AT l_instance_tab2 ASSIGNING <rec>.
    MOVE <rec>-instance TO l_instno_rec-instance.
    REFRESH l_instno_tab.
    INSERT l_instno_rec INTO TABLE l_instno_tab.

    create object lr_container.
    call method c_wa-r_engine->struc->read_level
      exporting
        i_ibase        = <rec>-com-ibase
                i_valfr        = l_sel_rec-p_valfr
                i_valto        = l_sel_rec-p_valto
                i_sort         = ibxx_false
                i_par          = l_par
                i_instance_tab = l_instno_tab
        ir_container   = lr_container.

    read table lr_container->gt_ibst into ls_ibst index 1.
    if sy-subrc is initial and ls_ibst-ibst-instance = <rec>-instance.
      read table lr_container->gth_ibstref into ls_ibstref with table key ibstref-st_recno = ls_ibst-ibst-st_recno.
      if sy-subrc is not initial. clear ls_ibstref. endif.
      perform com2_add_structure_data_det using <rec> ls_ibst ls_ibstref.
    ELSE.
      MESSAGE x096(iq).
    ENDIF. ]

4. **Error Conditions:**

- The error occurred under the following conditions: - [The sales demand transfer factory was changed from A to B]

- The error was caused by the following actions and inputs: - [An error occurred while operating a TECO production order using T-Code CO02 or COHV]

Please let me know if you need any additional information. Best regards, [wu]

entry.title: Combine selection and counting of values from database
entry.content:
Hi there,

I am looking for a solution to combine the selection of values from database and counting them in 1 SQL statement.

Unfortunately I don't get the solution so far by my own :(.

Example:

| matnr | text  |
-----------------
| 1     | test1 |
| 2     | test2 |
| 3     | test3 |

The preferred SELECT should lead to the following result:

| matnr | text  | count |
-------------------------
| 1     | test1 | 3     |
| 2     | test2 | 3     |
| 3     | test3 | 3     |

Any ideas?

BR

entry.title: Re: TM in SAP S/4HANA Public cloud
entry.content:
Hi Senthil,

SAP S/4HANA Public cloud offer the Transportation Management solution. We support the shipper scenarios well integrated with ERP process (refer the below blog for details) - You can refer the scope items for TM as per the blog - https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/transportation-management-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-2408/ba-p/13777054

For 2502 release the details will be published shortly.

Hope this is helpful. Please feel free to reachout of you have any further query.

Thanks and regards,

Amita Gupta

entry.title: Re: How to Track Approval Details of Maintenance Requests
entry.content: Hello, the rejected notifications will also have the history along with who made the status changes. Let me know if you have any issues otherwise. Thanks.

entry.title: Re: SAP Customer Data Cloud Back and Restore
entry.content:
Hi @Rvanjari ,

Good day.

As you are aware, there is no out-of-the-box functionality in CDC for Backup or Restore. But you can consider the following options for taking backup.

  1. Site Policies and Email Templates Backup:

    • You can back up all site policies and email templates using the accounts.getPolicies API.

  2. User Data Export:

    • You can periodically export user data using CDC's Data Access API or Bulk Data Extracts to create backups.
    • The exported data can be securely stored and used for restoration if required.

Regards,
Preethi G
SAP CDC Developer

Riya Shakya

But how that I am too figuring out. 

Let me know if you were able to achieve it.

entry.title: AI in SAP SuccessFactors: Revolutionizing HR with Transformative System Architecture
entry.content:
In today’s fast-evolving digital world, artificial intelligence (AI) is no longer just a futuristic concept; it's a transformative force in human resources (HR). SAP SuccessFactors, a leading cloud-based Human Capital Management (HCM) solution, has embraced AI technologies, reshaping how businesses manage talent, optimize operations, and make data-driven decisions. As AI continues to impact HR processes, the architecture of systems like SuccessFactors must evolve to support these innovations, enhancing scalability, integration, and security. This article explores how AI is integrated into SAP SuccessFactors and the resulting impact on its system architecture.

The Role of AI in SAP SuccessFactors

AI’s integration into SAP SuccessFactors is revolutionizing how businesses approach human capital management. By embedding AI across its key modules, SuccessFactors brings intelligent automation, advanced analytics, and predictive capabilities to HR practices. Key areas where AI plays a significant role include:

Recruitment & Talent Acquisition

AI-driven tools such as chatbots and machine learning algorithms are transforming the recruitment process. AI helps by screening resumes faster and more accurately, matching candidates to job profiles based on a wide range of variables. Predictive analytics also enable organizations to identify top candidates early in the process, streamlining hiring and improving candidate experience.

Employee Engagement & Development

AI’s ability to process vast amounts of data enables the development of personalized learning and development plans for employees. By assessing performance trends and individual strengths, AI can recommend customized career growth paths. Additionally, AI-powered sentiment analysis tools help measure employee engagement and satisfaction, providing actionable insights that HR leaders can use to foster a positive workplace culture.

Workforce Planning & Analytics

AI-powered analytics enable organizations to predict workforce trends, optimize resource allocation, and improve overall decision-making. These insights help HR departments plan for future workforce needs, identify potential skill gaps, and forecast employee turnover—all of which lead to more informed and effective workforce strategies.

AI-Powered Features in SuccessFactors

Several AI-driven features are already being used across SAP SuccessFactors, driving more efficient HR processes:

Intelligent Recruiting

SuccessFactors’ AI-based recruiting tools not only speed up resume screening but also help HR professionals find the best candidate matches. By leveraging natural language processing (NLP) and machine learning, AI can analyze candidate profiles and rank them based on fit with job requirements, ensuring that the right person is hired for the job.

People Analytics

SuccessFactors’ people analytics module uses AI to mine data from multiple sources, providing actionable insights about employee performance, engagement, and retention. For instance, AI models can predict which employees are at risk of leaving, allowing HR teams to intervene and reduce turnover. Additionally, AI-driven diversity and inclusion analytics help organizations ensure fair and equitable hiring practices.

Employee Experience

AI in SuccessFactors is not just about improving business processes; it’s about enhancing the employee experience. AI-driven recommendations suggest personalized training, career development opportunities, and well-being programs tailored to the individual, ensuring employees receive the resources they need to succeed.

Automated HR Operations

AI-powered automation eliminates repetitive HR tasks, such as payroll processing, attendance tracking, and leave management. By automating these functions, HR professionals can focus on more strategic activities while improving the accuracy and efficiency of administrative operations.

Impact of AI on System Architecture

AI brings significant changes to the underlying system architecture of SAP SuccessFactors. The introduction of AI capabilities requires the platform to be adaptable, scalable, and capable of handling large amounts of complex data. Let’s explore the architectural shifts brought about by AI:

Scalability

AI systems require massive data processing power, especially when it comes to training machine learning models. As AI technologies become increasingly integral to SuccessFactors, the system architecture must be designed to scale quickly and handle large volumes of real-time data. Cloud-based infrastructure provides the necessary elasticity to accommodate growing data needs and to support AI-driven capabilities at scale.

Integration with Existing Modules

SuccessFactors integrates AI seamlessly into its existing modules, such as Employee Central, Learning, and Performance Management. This enables a smooth transition for HR teams to adopt new features without disrupting existing workflows. The system’s architecture supports these integrations by using APIs and microservices, which allow the AI features to interact with other modules and enhance overall functionality.

Data Storage & Management

AI models in SuccessFactors rely heavily on vast amounts of data. This makes effective data storage and management crucial. The platform utilizes advanced cloud storage solutions and real-time data processing capabilities, ensuring that AI models can access and analyze data quickly and efficiently. Real-time data processing also ensures that AI-driven insights are delivered instantly, allowing HR leaders to make informed decisions on the fly.

Cloud Infrastructure & Performance

AI technologies demand high-performance computing power. The cloud infrastructure supporting SAP SuccessFactors is designed to offer low latency and high availability, ensuring that AI-powered features operate smoothly across global environments. Whether it’s predictive analytics or real-time insights, the cloud infrastructure enables businesses to harness the full potential of AI without compromising performance.

Security & Compliance

With AI’s reliance on sensitive employee data, ensuring robust security is paramount. SuccessFactors’ system architecture prioritizes data privacy and compliance with regional regulations, such as the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). AI-powered tools are designed with security measures to ensure that employee data remains protected and that AI-driven processes are compliant with legal frameworks.

The Future of AI in SuccessFactors

As AI continues to evolve, the future of SAP SuccessFactors looks promising. We can expect even more advanced predictive capabilities that help HR professionals anticipate employee needs, improve retention, and optimize workforce performance. AI-driven personalized career development and well-being programs could become more prevalent, ensuring a holistic approach to employee satisfaction.

Moreover, the ability to make real-time, data-driven decisions will only grow stronger, empowering HR departments to drive business outcomes faster and more effectively.

entry.title: How to hide some "screen" on MDG
entry.content:
Hello team, 

In the context of GDPR, I would like to hide the BANK ACCOUNT section based on a role assigned to the user.

To access MDG, I go through the Fiori launchpad.

Do you know if it is possible to hide certain screens based on roles in MDG? If so, how can it be done?

Thanks


PI : 
BS_BP_BANK_ACCOUNT id of UIBB of BANK ACCOUNT in web dynpro BS_SP_OVP

entry.title: Re: Editability of custom fields in SD_BILLINGDOC
entry.content:
Hello @nitin-jagtap08

we did not succeed. Finally I deactivated the fields on the custom fields tab, as they are anyways informed in the sales order and transferred to the billing document by copy control. In that case it's not possible to change the value anymore in the VFxx transactions, but that's better than having them ready for input in any case.

Regards
Sebastian

entry.title: Re: How to select managedStores from Employee type in Fle...
entry.content:
Example that you are describing is that you want to list relation objects and in case of relations they are not persisted within the item type itself but there is separate table created for it which you would need to query in order to get results.

To identify what type you would need to query you might need to check Employee type in Backoffice:

1. Enter Backoffice>Types>Employee>Properties
2. Enter Properties tab
3. Search for property named managedStores and click triple dot button>Edit details
4. If property is RelationDescriptor then search for "Relation Type" field and doubleclick it.
5. Use its identifier to run flexible search.

Note: In order to get results associated with certain employee, identify source and target item types and modify query by including employee pk in "where" clause.

entry.title: Re: HANA Studio on OSX - BW Modeling Issue
entry.content: in regards of not showing the unassigned navs of source providers, restarting Eclipse should solve the issue. quite old bug I've learned.

entry.title: Re: Combine selection and counting of values from database
entry.content: What did you try?

entry.title: Re: Hide fields in Table Maintenance generator ??
entry.content: My table is ZSVEMPLOYEE_1 and it has fields like ID,Name,School,College,DOJ. now I need to hide School field.  if im trying to do the above code i got an error like tctrl_zsvemployee_1-cols is unknown, but if trying to access in Debugging i am able to access that tctrl_zsvemployee_1-cols and i can pass values to Screen in debugging.

entry.title: Version data available in JVTO1 table in sap
entry.content:
Hello Team,

Good day

In JVTO1 table in sap which is JV Summary Table can we add new versions in there to fetch the data ?

We have received a query from business that they have loaded values in budget version TS2 which is for Group Budget reporting. However when they look in table JVTO1 those values do not appear and also we cannot see version TS2 in JVTO1 table under versions.

So kindly help to understand what types of version values can we see in JVTO1 table as business wants to know why some versions are available and some are not in JVTO1 table
